heading: Residential Energy Assistance Challenge option (R.E.A.Ch.)
label: 8626b Residential Energy Assistance Challenge option (R.E.A.Ch.)

children.text: minimize health and safety risks that result from high energy burdens on low-income Americans;
prevent homelessness as a result of inability to pay energy bills;
increase the efficiency of energy usage by low-income families; and
target energy assistance to individuals who are most in need.

children.heading: Allocation
children.label: 1 Allocation
children.text: For each fiscal year, the Secretary may allocate not more than 25 percent of the amount made available pursuant to for such fiscal year to a R.E.A.Ch. fund for the purpose of making incentive grants to States that submit qualifying plans that are approved by the Secretary as R.E.A.Ch. initiatives. States may use such grants for the costs of planning, implementing, and evaluating the initiative.section 8621(d) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: have energy efficiency education services plans that meet quality standards established by the Secretary in consultation with the Secretary of Energy; and

children.label: B
children.text: have the potential for being replicable model designs for other programs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall establish criteria for approving State plans required by subsection (a), for energy efficiency education quality standards described in subsection (b)(2)(A), and for the distribution of funds to States with approved plans.May 31, 1995

children.heading: Documentation
children.label: 2 Documentation
children.text: Notwithstanding the limitations of regarding the authority of the Secretary with respect to plans, the Secretary may require a State to provide appropriate documentation that its R.E.A.Ch. activities conform to the State plan as approved by the Secretary.section 8624(b) of this title

children.heading: Focus
children.label: d Focus
children.text: The State may designate all or part of the State, or all or part of the client population, as a focus of its R.E.A.Ch. initiative.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each State plan shall include each of the elements described in paragraph (2), to be met by State and local agencies.

children.label: i
children.text: awarding grants to, or entering into contracts with, such entities for the purpose of providing such services and payments directly to individuals eligible for benefits; or

children.label: I
children.text: determining eligibility;

children.label: II
children.text: providing outreach services; and

children.label: III
children.text: providing benefits other than payments;

children.label: i
children.text: are described in , except where significant geographic portions of the State are not served by such entities;section 9902(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary has determined have a record of successfully providing services under the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program; and

children.label: iii
children.text: receive weatherization assistance program funds under part A of title IV of the Energy Conservation and Production Act [ et seq.];42 U.S.C. 6861

children.label: i
children.text: payments to, or on behalf of, individuals eligible for residential energy assistance services and benefits under for home energy costs;section 8624(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: energy efficiency education;

children.label: iii
children.text: residential energy demand management services, including any other energy related residential repair and energy efficiency improvements in coordination with, or delivered by, Department of Energy weatherization assistance programs at the discretion of the State;

children.label: iv
children.text: family services, such as counseling and needs assessment, related to energy budget management, payment plans, and related services; and

children.label: v
children.text: negotiation with home energy suppliers on behalf of households eligible for R.E.A.Ch. services and benefits;

children.label: i
children.text: which households will receive one or more forms of benefits under the State R.E.A.Ch. initiative;

children.label: ii
children.text: the cases in which nonmonetary benefits are likely to provide more cost-effective long-term outcomes than payment benefits alone; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the amount of such benefit required to meet the goals of the program;

children.label: E
children.text: a method for targeting nonmonetary benefits;

children.label: i
children.text: discourage family energy crises;

children.label: ii
children.text: encourage responsible vendor and consumer behavior; and

children.label: iii
children.text: provide only financial incentives that encourage household payment;

children.label: i
children.text: provide incentives for recipients of assistance to pay home energy costs; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide incentives for vendors to help reduce the energy burdens of recipients of assistance;

children.label: H
children.text: an assurance that the State will require each entity that receives a grant or enters into a contract under this section to solicit and be responsive to the views of individuals who are financially eligible for benefits and services under this section in establishing its local program;

children.label: i
children.text: a reduction in the energy costs of participating households over one or more fiscal years;

children.label: ii
children.text: an increase in the regularity of home energy bill payments by eligible households; and

children.label: iii
children.text: an increase in energy vendor contributions towards reducing energy burdens of eligible households;

children.label: J
children.text: a description of the indicators that will be used by the State to measure whether the performance goals have been achieved;

children.label: K
children.text: a demonstration that the plan is consistent with , paragraphs (2), (3), (4), (5), (7), (9), (10), (11), (12), (13), and (14) of , subsections (d), (e), (f), (g), (h), (i), and (j) of , and ;section 8622 of this titlesection 8624(b) of this titlesection 8624 of this titlesection 8625 of this title

children.label: L
children.text: an assurance that benefits and services will be provided in addition to other benefit payments and services provided under this subchapter and in coordination with such benefit payments and services; and

children.label: M
children.text: an assurance that no regulated utility covered by the plan will be required to act in a manner that is inconsistent with applicable regulatory requirements.

children.heading: Cost or function
children.label: f Cost or function
children.text: None of the costs of providing services or benefits under this section shall be considered to be an administrative cost or function for purposes of any limitation on administrative costs or functions contained in this subchapter.
